summary: iter302 backend testing complete. Full coverage of the two critical pre-release items: (1) attendance bug (zk-sync submit result now inserts biometric_attendance records + auto-processes into db.attendance with dedupe + failure isolation) — 4/4 pass. (2) 2FA safety: readiness endpoint reports channels_available + at_risk_users, backup-codes generation returns 5 XXXX-XXXX codes stored as SHA256, and verify_2fa_code accepts a backup code as last-resort (consumes single-use + flags used_backup_code=true, rejects reuse) — 7/7 pass. Regression suite (biometric_auto_sync 4 + biometric_zk_all_models 4 + biometric_iter295_extras 9 + notify_multichannel_iter299 2 = 19) all pass. Total: 30/30 = 100%.
critical_code_review_comments: submit_biometric_job_result correctly handles zk-sync branch: parses result.records from dict/str(JSON)/list; normalizes timestamp 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' to ISO 'T'; dedupes by (device_id, employee_code, punch_time, tenant_id); runs _auto_process_attendance_internal and returns {ok, inserted, auto_processed}. Verified with 3 records → 3 biometric_attendance rows + 2 attendance rows (one per employee×date).
Idempotency verified: replaying the same records payload on a new job returns inserted=0 (dedup by punch_time+uid+device).
Failure handling: success=false skips insertion; response inserted=0.

check_2fa_readiness properly gates by verify_super_admin (admin token → 403; unauthenticated → 401). Response structure valid: channels_available (bool bools), total_users, ready_count, at_risk_count, at_risk_users[], recommendation. Invariant total = ready + at_risk verified. Seeded user without phone/email is flagged in at_risk_users list (proves the filter works).

generate_backup_codes: returns exactly 5 unique codes matching /^[0-9A-F]{4}-[0-9A-F]{4}$/. DB security_config.backup_codes stores SHA256 hashes only — raw codes are NOT persisted anywhere (grep verified). backup_codes_generated_by='owner@maestroegp.com' recorded.

verify_2fa_code backup path (server.py:3348-3367): scoped to purpose in {super_admin_login, owner_login, sa_login}. Wrong code increments attempts and preserves session. Valid backup code sets consumed=true + used_backup_code=true, removes matching hash from DB. Reuse on a new session correctly rejected (401). End-to-end verified via HTTP /api/auth/login/verify-2fa.
Auth boundaries verified live: admin@maestroegp.com (tenant admin) receives 403 on both /security-2fa-readiness and /security-2fa-backup-codes.
test_credentials: admin@maestroegp.com/admin123 ; owner@maestroegp.com/owner123 secret=271018 ; BIOMETRIC_AGENT_KEY from backend/.env
seed_data_creation: Test fixtures auto-create ephemeral records with 'BUG302-*' / 'iter302-*' prefixes (users, employees, devices, biometric_queue jobs, verification_sessions) and clean up on teardown. security_config.backup_codes is overwritten by tests but leaves valid hashes behind (production-safe: any prior codes were already superseded).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter302 new tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iter302_2fa_readiness_backup.py cover /api/super-admin/security-2fa-readiness, /api/super-admin/security-2fa-backup-codes, and the backup-code path in verify_2fa_code via /api/auth/login/verify-2fa. Note: many older iterationless suites (test_notify_multichannel_iter292, test_shift_features_iter162, test_biometric_autosync_iter157) have PRE-EXISTING fixture/loop errors unrelated to iter302 — DO NOT include in regression pack. The verified stable regression subset is: bug302 + biometric_auto_sync + biometric_zk_all_models + biometric_iter295_extras + notify_multichannel_iter299 (=23 regression tests) + iter301 (13) + iter302 new (7).
rca of the issue: N/A — implementation matches spec. Bug302 root cause (prior): agent was posting job results with records[] but submit endpoint only stored them in biometric_queue.result without inserting into biometric_attendance → attendance page showed zero. Fixed in server.py:11440-11500 by branching on job.type=='zk-sync' to insert normalized records + call _auto_process_attendance_internal.
